name: ghidra
description: Reverse engineering via Ghidra CLI (akiselev/ghidra-cli) — headless analysis, decompilation, function/symbol/type editing, cross-refs, search, patching, and scripting from the terminal.
prerequisites: Ghidra 10+ install (or run `ghidra setup` after install)

install.notes: ghidra-cli is a Rust CLI not yet published to crates.io; install from the GitHub source. Requires JDK 17+ and a Ghidra install. After install, run `ghidra setup` to auto-download Ghidra, or set GHIDRA_INSTALL_DIR / `ghidra config set ghidra_install_dir <path>`.

integration_test.description: Verifies CLI sees Ghidra install and a working JDK.

post_install_hints: Install a JDK 17+ if not present (Windows: `winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.21`; macOS: `brew install openjdk@21`; Linux: `sudo apt install openjdk-21-jdk`).
Install Ghidra: either run `ghidra setup` to auto-download, or download from https://ghidra-sre.org and set GHIDRA_INSTALL_DIR (or `ghidra config set ghidra_install_dir <path>`).
Verify with `ghidra doctor` — it checks the Ghidra install, JDK, and project directory.
